action.reasoning: Observation: Best test Spearman is 0.7432 from basic MLP. Additive residual approach failed because NN corrections didn't generalize to >2 mutations. Current model (iter 17) hasn't been tested yet. Analysis: For extrapolation from ≤2 to >2 mutations, we should trust the additive model more for higher mutation counts since it naturally extends. The MLP likely degrades for out-of-distribution sequences. Decision: (1) Submit current model eval to get baseline, (2) Modify predict.py to blend MLP + additive predictions with mutation-count-dependent alpha, (3) Also add ridge regression as an additional model for better extrapolation. Start with the blending approach since it doesn't require retraining.
